1. Introduction  
   1. puts  
   2. varName \=  
   3. convention is uppercase lettters are avoided and variables are in snake case  
      1. NOT  
         1. varName  
      2. OK  
         1. var\_name  
   4. can reassign variables to different types  
   5. Math  
      1. integer literal is written as a series of 1 or mor base digits without leading zeros, default type is int32  
      2. same goes for floating point, just need 2 or more digits with a . in there  
      3. basic comparison operators  
         1. lt, gt, lte, gte  
         2. SPACESHIP OPERATOR  
            1. 0 if equal  
            2. \+ if 1st is bigger  
            3. \- if 2nd is bigger  
      4. operations  
         1. basics:  
            1. add  
            2. subtract  
            3. multiply  
            4. exponentiation \*\*  
            5. division  
            6. floor division  
            7. modulus  
            8. negation  
            9. \==  
            10. \!=  
         2. advanced  
            1. inversion  
            2. binary complement  
            3. shl append  
            4. shr  
            5. and  
            6. or  
            7. xor  
            8. splat  
               1. like \*args and \*\*kwargs in python  
            9. ternary  
               1. a ? x : y  
            10. inclusive range/exclusive range  
                1. ..  
                2. …  
            11. logical and  
            12. logical or  
                1. &&  
                2. ||  
            13. pattern match operator  
            14. no pattern match operator  
            15. case subsumption operator  
                1. does the lhs contain or describe the stuff on the rhs?  
      5. math has useful functions and constants using the namespace :: operator  
   6. strings  
      1. \#{} string interpolation  
         1. alternative is concatenation  
      2. more complex values should be done witih .to\_s  
      3. multiline strings delimited by \\ in or outside the string  
      4. HEREDOC  
         1. here document can be useful for writing strings spanning over multiple lines  
         2. SO MUCH BETTER  
2. Terms  
   1. splats, splat expansions  
      1. single/double splats  
      2. sets {}  
      3. hash, hash-like  
      4. named-tuple //basically json-like  
      5. proc //assign a function to a variable  
      6. command // \`echo foo\`, %x(echo foo)  
   2. switch, case \= case…when  
   3. select…when…end  
      1. select expression chooses from a set of non-blocking operations and proceeds with the branch that becomes available first  
      2. select action as non-blocking  
      3. select actions call a method with an implicit suffix ?, and returns an instance of the select action  
      4. if one is available it immediately returns, otherwise waits for completion  
         1. as soon as one branch completes all others are canceled  
   4. assignment at break for loops  
      1. var \= \<loop\> break "${return value}" if cond \== val  
         1. \<return var\>= \<loop\>  
   5. self restriction  
      1. type restriction is the same as writing the class  
      2. can restrict based on class types as everything is an object in crystal  
         1. x : \<Type\>.class  
      3. type restriction can be used with splats  
   6. method definition  
      1. types:  
         1. required/optional positional parameters  
         2. optional splat parameter  
         3. required and optional named parameters  
         4. optional double splat parameter  
      2. if there's a splat in between, the resulting named argument must have a named positional parameter in it  
   7. type reflection  
      1. see the type at runtime/compile time  
         1. is\_a  
            1. boolean typeof()  
         2. nil  
         3. responds\_to  
            1. whether the type has a method that has that name  
         4. as  
            1. restricts the type union to that type  
            2. useful for casting to a larger type  
            3.   
         5. as?  
            1. same as before but with nil available  
         6. typeof() returns the type of an expression  
   8. type autocasting  
      1. casts to a larger type if no precision is lost  
      2. if there's ambiguity compiler throws an error:  
         1. ![][image1]

Keywords:

1. control flow  
   1. if, else, end, unless, while, next, break, case, when  
2. loops  
   1. select, unless, while, loop  
3. value type  
   1. true,false, nil  
4. non-standard operators  
   1. ⇔  
5. stdout  
   1. puts, p\! (this one does debug info too)  
6. functions  
   1. def \<module\_if\_available\>.name (params) : returnType  
7. class/namespace  
   1. :: (namespace operator)  
   2. module  
8. require "\<filepath\>"  
   1. looks in standard library that comes with the compiler  
   2. lib directory relative to the cwd  
   3. require "./\<filename\>"  
   4. require "./folder/\*"   
      1. all .cr files in that containing folder  
   5. require "./folder/\*\*"   
      1. all .cr files recursively from that location  
9. classes  
   1. class  
      1. new initialize allocate  
         1. instantiates the class  
            1. //person \= Person.new  
         2. constructor  
            1. @\<varName\> to put on a class variable  
      2. previous\_def  
         1. calls previous definition of the class method  
         2. without args or parantheses, previous\_def receives all the current method/parameters as arguments  
            1. otherwise it receives the arguments you pass to it  
      3. initialize variables outside "initialize" method  
         1. @age \= 0  
      4. type inference  
         1. literal value  
         2. invoking the class method  
         3. def initialize (@name : String)  
         4. class method return type restriction  
            1. @name : String   
         5. class method return type restriction with default value  
            1. @name \= "John"  
         6. result of invoking lib function  
         7. 'out' lib function  
   2. union types   
      1. a : Int32 | String  
      2. when assigning a variable with options within the same class hierarchy, it will assign the type as ParentClass+  
   3. method overloading  
      1. \# of parameters  
      2. type restrictions applied to parameters  
      3. names of required named parameters  
      4. whether the method accepts a 'block' or not  
   4. pretty loose named argument calls and default parameter values  
10. forall T  
    1. type restriction based on T  
    2. def foo(x: T) forall T  
    3. when doing a splat with named tuple, you have to do var: with no space before the colon  
11. external\_name internal\_name  
    1. better for readability  
12. operator overloading  
    1. can define with:  
       1. def \<operator\> : self  
13. combined assignments  
    1. ||= is equivalent to a || (a=b)  
14. private and protected method types  
    1. private methods are visible by subclasses  
       1. and the base class that it's defined on  
    2. protected method can only be invoked on   
       1. instances of the same type as the current type  
       2. instances in the same namespace (class, struct, modle, etc) as the current type  
    3. private top level  
       1. only visible in the current file  
15. inheritance  
    1. \< operator  
    2. override methods in a derived class  
    3. invoke superclass' method using 'super'  
    4. initialize types supercede class @var types  
16. virtual/abstract types  
    1. virtual types are combining different types under the same class hierarchy  
       1. for instance when you do a class  
       2. ![][image2]  
    2. abstract classes are exactly that, never instantiated  
17. Class variables  
    1. bound to class,not instance "@@\<varName"  
    2. subclasses type is the same but each class has a different runtime value  
18. finalize  
    1. when an instance of that class is garbage-collected that method will be invoked  
    2. basically class destructor (crystal has a GC)  
19. modules  
    1. basically namespaces for defining other types, methods, and cosntants  
    2. partial types that can be mixed with other types  
    3. INCLUDE includes a module's methods in that module as instance methods  
    4. EXTEND does include but as class methods  
       1. extend self can put that own module's namespace in there idk  
20. Generics  
    1. ![][image3]  
    2. good for ADTs  
       1. ![][image4]  
21. struct  
    1. instead of defining a type with class you can do so with struct  
    2. always passed by value even when you return 'self' from the method of that struct  
    3. structs cannot inherit from a non-abstract struct  
22. Records  
    1. struct macro to simplify the definition of basic struct types with an initializer and some helper methods  
    2. ![][image5]  
23. CONSTANTS  
    1. ![][image6]  
24. enum  
    1. exactly how you think, override default value with assignment operator  
    2. flags enums  
       1. @\[Flags\] annotation makes the enums a power of 2   
    3. can define methods for enums  
25. do…end OR while …  
    1. can use to execute that block of code as denoted by & in the function parameters  
    2. ![][image7]  
    3. basically works like a loop but for macro function expansions  
26. block invocation  
    1. ![][image8]  
27. alias different name  
28. raise  
    1. exception  
    2. raise Exception.new()  
    3. inherit from Exception to make a custom exception type  
    4. begin … rescue … else … ensure … end  
       1. try catch basically  
       2. with else and ensure to make sure something happens in any of the cases  
29. macro  
    1. these receive ast nodes at compile-time and produce code that is pasted into a program  
       1. ![][image9]  
    2. declared at top level, if marked private its only accessible in that file  
    3. Macro language is different than crystal  
       1. subset of crystal's features  
    4. basically one step up for metaprogramming  
    5. different dialect of crystal  
       1. used for evaluation at compile time not at runtime like blocks/procs  
    6. macros cannot be commented out  
    7. macro hooks  
       1. ![][image10]  
       2. stacking  
       3. overriding  
       4.   
30. verbatim  
    1. allows for direct copy/paste of the macro code with explicit bindings pass inside that block  
31. annotation  
    1. add metadata to certain features in the source code  
       1. ![][image11]  
       2. great for storing metadata about a given whatever to read at compile time using macros  
       3. built-in annotations:  
          1. Link \- tell slinker how to linka  c library  
          2. Extern \-   
             1. marking a crystal struct with this annot makes it possible to use in lib declarations  
          3. ThreadLocal  
             1. makes class variables/c external variables thread local  
          4. Packed  
             1. prevents c structs from becoming padded  
          5. AlwaysInline  
          6. NoInline  
          7. ReturnsTwice  
          8. Raises  
             1. possibly returns an exception  
          9. CallConvention  
    2. low level primitives  
       1. ![][image12]  
    3. c bindings  
       1. lib, fun, struct, union, enum, variables, constants, type, alias, callbacks

Style Guide:

1. variables are snake case  
2. method names begin with a lowercase letter and as a convention, only use lowercase letters, underscores, and numbers

Crystal quirks:

1. linker that's used by default doesn't work at significant size  
2. \=== is standard equality \+ type  
   1. called "case equality operator"  
   2. ![][image13]
